title: crataegus × macrocarpa nothovar. calycina
intro: Crataegus × macrocarpa nothovar. calycina is a hybrid plant species of hawthorn, which is a cross between the Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its flavonoids, triterpenes, and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which contribute to its health benefits. Consuming the plant or its extracts has been associated with reduced blood pressure, improved cardiovascular health, and increased antioxidant activity. The herbal preparations of Crataegus × macrocarpa nothovar. calycina can be found in various forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
